frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: system dynamically reallocates encoding resources based on environmental statistics

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: prior information integrated to optimally estimate current state in motor planning

core_question: How does the motor system leverage short-term priors from previous movements when planning the current reach in unpredictable environments?
key_assumptions: encoding resources can be dynamically reallocated
intrinsic motor variability interacts with sequential effects
key_findings: current movements were biased opposite to the direction of previous movements
movement variability decreased when successive reaches were similar
sequential bias is enhanced by intrinsic motor variability
sequential effects contradicted Bayesian models and aligned with efficient coding
interpretations: motor planning uses efficient coding rather than Bayesian integration to exploit short-term priors
the motor system dynamically reallocates encoding resources based on recent movement statistics
